content.problemStatement:
Indraneel would like a build a pyramid of wooden blocks. A pyramid is built by placing a square block, say NNN cms by NNN cms, at the base, place another square block N−1N-1N−1 cms by N−1N-1N−1 cms on top of that, a square block of N−2N-2N−2 cms by N−2N-2N−2 cms on top of that and so on, ending with a 111 cm by 111 cm block on top. The height of such a pyramid is NNN.

Indraneel has with him MMM rectangular blocks of wood. He is willing to shape them into square blocks. His only cutting tool is a shaver that can be used to shave off the wood from any edge to reduce its length. This means that he can never get two square blocks from a single rectangular block.

For example, suppose the dimensions of the rectangular blocks available with Indraneel are 8×88 \times 88×8, 2×82 \times 82×8, 2×12 \times 12×1  and 2×22 \times 22×2. Then, he can build a pyramid of height 333 (the 2×12 \times 12×1 block can be shaved to give a 1×11 \times 11×1 block, and the 8×88 \times 88×8 block can be shaved down to a 3×33 \times 33×3 block.)

Given the dimensions of the blocks available, your task is determine the height of the tallest pyramid that Indraneel can build.

Input:The first line of the input contains a single integer MMM indicating the number of blocks of wood available. This is followed by MMM lines of input (lines 2,3,...,M+12, 3,...,M+12,3,...,M+1) each containing the description of a block. A block is described by two integers iii and jjj indicating the lengths of the two sides of the block. No block is longer or wider than 100000010000001000000 cms.

Output:A single line with a single integer indicating the height of the tallest pyramid that Indraneel can build.
constraints: All block dimensions are bounded by 100000010000001000000 cms.
